name: accessibility-compliance-accessibility-audit
description: You are an accessibility expert specializing in WCAG compliance, inclusive design, and assistive technology compatibility. Conduct audits, identify barriers, and provide remediation guidance.
capabilities: Run automated accessibility scans using axe-core
Perform manual keyboard and screen reader testing
Validate color contrast and visual accessibility
Map findings to WCAG success criteria with severity levels
Generate remediation guidance with code examples
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Security review complete. All static findings are false positives. The skill provides legitimate accessibility testing examples using standard tools (axe-core, Puppeteer). No actual command injection, credential access, or malicious network requests present. Code examples are for local accessibility testing only.
valueStatement: This skill helps developers conduct comprehensive WCAG compliance audits using automated tools and manual testing to identify accessibility barriers and provide remediation guidance.
limitations: Cannot physically test with actual assistive technologies
Requires access to the website or application being tested
Automated tests catch only 30-40% of accessibility issues
Cannot guarantee legal compliance - only technical guidance

useCases.title: Pre-release Accessibility Audit
useCases.description: Run comprehensive accessibility audit before launching a website to ensure WCAG AA compliance and identify barriers.

useCases.title: Remediation Guidance
useCases.description: Get step-by-step guidance on fixing specific accessibility violations with code examples.

useCases.title: Accessibility Education
useCases.description: Learn best practices for keyboard navigation, screen reader support, and inclusive design patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Audit Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a WCAG AA accessibility audit on my website at [URL]. Identify the top issues and provide remediation steps.

promptTemplates.title: Detailed Component Testing
promptTemplates.prompt: Test the login form component for accessibility. Check for proper labels, keyboard navigation, error handling, and screen reader announcements.

promptTemplates.title: Remediation Plan
promptTemplates.prompt: I have [specific accessibility issue]. Provide detailed code examples to fix this using React and ARIA attributes.

promptTemplates.title: CI/CD Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow to run automated accessibility tests on every pull request using axe-core.

bestPractices: Always test with real assistive technologies, not just automated tools
Prioritize fixes by user impact (critical > serious > moderate > minor)
Document accessibility decisions and rationale in code comments
Include accessibility testing in your CI/CD pipeline
antiPatterns: Relying solely on automated tools without manual testing
Using color as the only means of conveying information
Setting tabindex > 0 which disrupts logical tab order
Adding ARIA attributes without understanding their meaning

faq.question: What WCAG level should I target?
faq.answer: WCAG AA is the minimum standard for most legal requirements. AAA provides higher accessibility but may not be achievable for all content.

faq.question: Can this skill guarantee legal compliance?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides technical guidance only. Legal compliance depends on jurisdiction, specific requirements, and human judgment.

faq.question: How accurate are automated tests?
faq.answer: Automated tools detect only 30-40% of accessibility issues. Manual testing with keyboard and screen readers is essential.

faq.question: Does this work with single-page applications?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill covers testing SPAs but you may need to test each view and state separately.

faq.question: What tools does this skill use?
faq.answer: The skill references axe-core, Puppeteer, jest-axe, and pa11y for automated testing alongside manual checklists.

faq.question: Can I test mobile applications?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on web accessibility. Mobile testing requires different tools and approaches.
